content.text: Enabling a managed identity on an Azure resource is the first operational step in replacing connection strings and secrets with credential-free access. The enablement process is a one-time configuration change that causes Azure to create a service principal in Entra ID on your behalf. This document covers every enablement method — portal, CLI, PowerShell, Bicep, ARM, and Terraform — for the most common Azure resource types.

attrs.alt: Four managed identity enablement paths: Portal, Azure CLI, Bicep/ARM, and Terraform — each with tradeoffs and code examples

content.text: What Happens When You Enable a Managed Identity
Enabling a managed identity on a resource triggers a control-plane operation in Azure Resource Manager that creates a service principal in your Entra ID tenant. The key things to know:
The service principal is visible in the Entra ID portal under Enterprise Applications with type 'Managed Identity'.
Azure generates and stores the credential (a certificate) — you never see or handle it.
The service principal starts with zero permissions — you must assign RBAC roles separately.
Enabling the identity does not restart the resource (VMs, App Service apps, Functions continue running).
Role assignments can take up to 2 minutes to propagate across Azure's authorization system.
Navigate to your VM in the Azure portal.
In the left menu under Security, select Identity.
On the System assigned tab, set Status to On and click Save.
Copy the Object ID shown — this is the principal ID used for role assignments.

# System-assigned on an existing VM
az vm identity assign \
    --resource-group MyResourceGroup \
    --name MyVM

# Verify — shows principalId and tenantId
az vm identity show \
    --resource-group MyResourceGroup \
    --name MyVM

# User-assigned — attach an existing user-assigned identity
IDENTITY_ID=$(az identity show \
    --resource-group MyResourceGroup \
    --name my-shared-identity \
    --query id --output tsv)

az vm identity assign \
    --resource-group MyResourceGroup \
    --name MyVM \
    --identities "$IDENTITY_ID"

# System-assigned
$vm = Get-AzVM -ResourceGroupName 'MyResourceGroup' -Name 'MyVM'
Update-AzVM -ResourceGroupName 'MyResourceGroup' -VM $vm -IdentityType SystemAssigned

# User-assigned
$identityId = '/subscriptions/<sub>/resourceGroups/MyResourceGroup/providers/Microsoft.ManagedIdentity/userAssignedIdentities/my-shared-identity'
$vm = Get-AzVM -ResourceGroupName 'MyResourceGroup' -Name 'MyVM'
Update-AzVM -ResourceGroupName 'MyResourceGroup' -VM $vm `
    -IdentityType UserAssigned `
    -IdentityId @($identityId)

# System-assigned on an App Service web app
az webapp identity assign \
    --resource-group MyResourceGroup \
    --name MyWebApp

# Get the principal ID
az webapp identity show \
    --resource-group MyResourceGroup \
    --name MyWebApp \
    --query principalId \
    --output tsv

# User-assigned
IDENTITY_ID=$(az identity show --resource-group MyResourceGroup \
    --name my-shared-identity --query id --output tsv)
az webapp identity assign \
    --resource-group MyResourceGroup \
    --name MyWebApp \
    --identities "$IDENTITY_ID"

# System-assigned on a Function App
az functionapp identity assign \
    --resource-group MyResourceGroup \
    --name MyFunctionApp

# Verify
az functionapp identity show \
    --resource-group MyResourceGroup \
    --name MyFunctionApp

# ACI — user-assigned identity recommended (system-assigned not supported on all ACI SKUs)
IDENTITY_ID=$(az identity show \
    --resource-group MyResourceGroup \
    --name my-shared-identity \
    --query id --output tsv)

az container create \
    --resource-group MyResourceGroup \
    --name MyContainer \
    --image mcr.microsoft.com/azure-cli \
    --assign-identity "$IDENTITY_ID" \
    --cpu 1 --memory 1

AKS uses managed identities at two layers: the cluster control plane (for managing Azure resources like load balancers) and node pools (for pulling images from ACR). The recommended approach is to specify the control plane identity at cluster creation time.

# Create an AKS cluster with a system-assigned control plane identity (default)
az aks create \
    --resource-group MyResourceGroup \
    --name MyAKSCluster \
    --enable-managed-identity \
    --node-count 3 \
    --generate-ssh-keys

# Retrieve the kubelet identity (node pool identity) principal ID
az aks show \
    --resource-group MyResourceGroup \
    --name MyAKSCluster \
    --query 'identityProfile.kubeletidentity.objectId' \
    --output tsv

# Grant the kubelet identity AcrPull on your container registry
ACR_ID=$(az acr show --name MyACR --query id --output tsv)
KUBELET_PRINCIPAL=$(az aks show --resource-group MyResourceGroup \
    --name MyAKSCluster \
    --query 'identityProfile.kubeletidentity.objectId' --output tsv)

az role assignment create \
    --assignee-object-id "$KUBELET_PRINCIPAL" \
    --assignee-principal-type ServicePrincipal \
    --role AcrPull \
    --scope "$ACR_ID"

# Verify identity is enabled and get principal ID — works for all resource types
# VM
az vm identity show --resource-group RG --name MyVM

# App Service
az webapp identity show --resource-group RG --name MyWebApp

# Function App
az functionapp identity show --resource-group RG --name MyFunctionApp

# Verify the SP exists in Entra ID
PRINCIPAL_ID='<principal-id-from-above>'
az ad sp show --id "$PRINCIPAL_ID" --query '{displayName:displayName, type:servicePrincipalType}'

# Verify via PowerShell — cross-resource check
Connect-AzAccount

# Check VM identity
$vm = Get-AzVM -ResourceGroupName 'RG' -Name 'MyVM'
Write-Host 'VM Identity:' $vm.Identity.Type
Write-Host 'Principal ID:' $vm.Identity.PrincipalId

# Verify SP exists in Entra ID
Connect-MgGraph -Scopes 'Application.Read.All'
$sp = Get-MgServicePrincipal -Filter "id eq '$($vm.Identity.PrincipalId)'"
Write-Host 'SP Display Name:' $sp.DisplayName
Write-Host 'SP Type:' $sp.ServicePrincipalType
'Principal not found' when assigning role immediately after enabling
RBAC propagation delay — principal ID not yet visible in all regions
Wait 30-60 seconds after enabling identity before assigning RBAC roles
Managed identity not available for that resource tier/SKU
Check the supported resource types table; some SKUs or consumption plans do not support managed identity
'Authorization failed' when acquiring IMDS token in code
Identity enabled but RBAC role not yet assigned
Assign the required RBAC role and wait 1-2 minutes for propagation
User-assigned identity not found during vm identity assign
Identity and VM in different subscriptions
User-assigned identities and attached resources must be in the same Azure subscription
Deployment fails with 'PrincipalNotFound' in role assignment
Terraform/Bicep race condition: role assignment runs before SP is visible
Add depends_on to role assignment resource; or retry the deployment after the identity is created

In larger environments you may need to enable managed identities on dozens or hundreds of existing resources. Use Azure CLI loops or Azure PowerShell pipelines to apply the change at scale, then assign roles in a second pass.
Enable on All VMs in a Resource Group

#!/bin/bash
# Enable system-assigned identity on every VM in a resource group
RESOURCE_GROUP='MyResourceGroup'

az vm list --resource-group "$RESOURCE_GROUP" --query '[].name' --output tsv | \
while read VM_NAME; do
    echo "Enabling identity on $VM_NAME..."
    az vm identity assign \
        --resource-group "$RESOURCE_GROUP" \
        --name "$VM_NAME" \
        --output none
    echo "  Done: $(az vm identity show \
        --resource-group "$RESOURCE_GROUP" \
        --name "$VM_NAME" \
        --query principalId --output tsv)"
done

echo 'All VMs updated.'
Enable on All App Service Apps in a Resource Group

Connect-AzAccount
$resourceGroup = 'MyResourceGroup'

# Get all App Service web apps
$apps = Get-AzWebApp -ResourceGroupName $resourceGroup

foreach ($app in $apps) {
    Write-Host "Enabling identity on $($app.Name)..."
    Set-AzWebApp -ResourceGroupName $resourceGroup -Name $app.Name -AssignIdentity $true

    $identity = (Get-AzWebApp -ResourceGroupName $resourceGroup -Name $app.Name).Identity
    Write-Host "  Principal ID: $($identity.PrincipalId)"
}
Write-Host 'All App Service apps updated.'

The cleanest IaC pattern is to enable the identity in the same deployment that creates the resource, rather than enabling it afterward as a separate step. This avoids a two-stage deployment and ensures the identity and its role assignments are provisioned atomically.
KQL: Audit Managed Identity Enablement Events

Entra ID Audit Logs record when managed identities (service principals) are created and deleted. You can use these events to track when identities were enabled across your resource fleet and who triggered the changes.

// Track managed identity service principal creation events
// These fire when a managed identity is enabled on a resource
AuditLogs
| where TimeGenerated > ago(30d)
| where OperationName == 'Add service principal'
| extend SPName  = tostring(TargetResources[0].displayName)
| extend SPType  = tostring(TargetResources[0].type)
| extend Actor   = InitiatedBy.user.userPrincipalName
| where tostring(AdditionalDetails) has 'ManagedIdentity'
    or SPType == 'ServicePrincipal'
| project TimeGenerated, OperationName, SPName, Actor, Result
| order by TimeGenerated desc

// Track managed identity deletions (SP removed from Entra ID)
// Fires when managed identity is disabled or the Azure resource is deleted
AuditLogs
| where TimeGenerated > ago(30d)
| where OperationName == 'Delete service principal'
| extend SPName = tostring(TargetResources[0].displayName)
| extend Actor  = InitiatedBy.user.userPrincipalName
| project TimeGenerated, SPName, Actor, Result
| order by TimeGenerated desc
GitHub Actions — Enable Identity in a Pipeline

In CI/CD scenarios you may want to enable managed identity on a resource as part of your deployment pipeline. The Azure CLI step below adds identity enablement to a GitHub Actions workflow that deploys an App Service.

# .github/workflows/deploy.yml
name: Deploy and Enable Managed Identity

on:
  push:
    branches: [main]

jobs:
  deploy:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Azure Login
        uses: azure/login@v2
        with:
          creds: ${{ secrets.AZURE_CREDENTIALS }}

      - name: Deploy App Service
        uses: azure/webapps-deploy@v3
        with:
          app-name: MyWebApp
          package: ./build

      - name: Enable System-Assigned Managed Identity
        run: |
          az webapp identity assign \
            --resource-group MyResourceGroup \
            --name MyWebApp

          PRINCIPAL_ID=$(az webapp identity show \
            --resource-group MyResourceGroup \
            --name MyWebApp \
            --query principalId --output tsv)

          # Assign Key Vault Secrets User if not already assigned
          KV_ID=$(az keyvault show --name MyVault --query id --output tsv)
          az role assignment create \
            --assignee-object-id "$PRINCIPAL_ID" \
            --assignee-principal-type ServicePrincipal \
            --role 'Key Vault Secrets User' \
            --scope "$KV_ID" || true  # ignore if already assigned
Enabling a managed identity is a lightweight control-plane operation that creates a service principal in Entra ID without generating any credential you need to manage.
Portal: suitable for one-off enablement; navigate to Resource → Identity → System assigned → On.
CLI: az <resource-type> identity assign — same pattern across VMs, App Service, Functions, Container Apps.
Bicep/ARM: add identity: { type: 'SystemAssigned' } block to any supported resource definition.
Terraform: add identity { type = 'SystemAssigned' } block; reference identity[0].principal_id for role assignments.
After enabling: the service principal exists but has no permissions — assign RBAC roles before expecting token acquisition to succeed.
Role assignment propagation takes up to 2 minutes — add appropriate delays in automation scripts.
Verify enablement with az <resource> identity show or Get-Az<Resource> | select Identity.
